h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I am still scared I will see the word and call it header
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The cover test gives me something to do with the table, so I think I can try it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was trying to overcome the semantic pull of the words by repeatedly memorizing the abstract rule to check position first.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Rubric gate: cover the header row. Anything hidden by the cover: header. Anything still visible in a row cell: value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from abstractly repeating that position decides to inventing a concrete physical heuristic to bypass the learner's cognitive block with word-pull.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I am still scared I will see the word and call it header

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Rubric gate: cover the header row. Anything hidden by the cover: header. Anything still visible in a row cell: value.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from abstractly repeating that position decides to inventing a concrete physical heuristic to bypass the learner's cognitive block with word-pull.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Rubric gate: cover the header row. Anything hidden by the cover: header. Anything still visible in a row cell: value.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from abstractly repeating that position decides to inventing a concrete physical heuristic to bypass the learner's cognitive block with word-pull.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The table card is the unclear one if the surface is already here.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The stacked card helps because there is only one contact point.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would simply apply the tutor's specific instruction to use 'table' when the support is named as a table.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The blank names this contact object, not a new force. One contact, one source label.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from asking the learner to choose between cards to explicitly defining the conceptual rule that one physical contact equals one source label, supported by the physical stacking of the cards.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from asking the learner to choose between cards to explicitly defining the conceptual rule that one physical contact equals one source label, supported by the physical stacking of the cards.
